text.title: Egress Cost Hidden Budget Guide Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Egress Cost Hidden Budget Guide methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every egress path MUST be enumerated: internet egress per service, cross-AZ per service, NAT-gateway processing, VPC peering, transit gateway. Path graph attached to the report.
rule.rationale: Hidden paths cause bill surprises; enumeration surfaces them before launch.

rule.statement: Each egress source MUST be attributed to a feature / customer-tier / job type. 'platform overhead' is not an attribution; trace to the feature.
rule.rationale: Without attribution, optimisations target the wrong feature; with attribution, the costly feature surfaces.

rule.statement: Per-region egress cap MUST be set with alert at 80% + freeze action at 100%. Cap calibrated to product margin (e.g. egress / revenue ratio ceiling).
rule.rationale: Caps stop runaway spend; alert + freeze enforce them.

rule.statement: Egress model MUST include worst-case (4x peak) traffic scenario; report shows what egress + cost would be at the spike. Reserved budget covers the spike.
rule.rationale: Without worst-case, the spike triggers freeze actions or bill panic.

rule.statement: When NAT-GW processing > $1k/month, evaluate alternatives: VPC endpoints for S3/DynamoDB, NAT instance for low volume, or direct internet via public subnet for non-private resources.
rule.rationale: NAT-GW processing is per-GB; VPC endpoints are free. Many setups overspend on NAT-GW by 10x.

rule.statement: Egress budget MUST be reviewed quarterly + per release with measurable traffic delta. Reviews update budgets + alerts.
rule.rationale: Traffic drifts; quarterly review keeps budget calibrated.
